Specialized roles (spawn fresh; the engine orchestrates)

WHEN any dev-spec-kit command runs inside a dev-spec-kit project THEN the system SHALL append a `cli.run` event (command path + arguments + timestamp) to the append-only journal `.dev-spec-kit/journal.jsonl`.

nodes.id: test:test/cli-ux.test.ts::audits cli invocations into the journal
nodes.label: test/cli-ux.test.ts::audits cli invocations into the journal
IF dev-spec-kit runs outside a dev-spec-kit project THEN the system SHALL NOT write any audit event.

nodes.id: test:test/cli-ux.test.ts::does not create journals outside dev-spec-kit projects
nodes.label: test/cli-ux.test.ts::does not create journals outside dev-spec-kit projects

WHEN `dev-spec-kit log` runs THEN the system SHALL print the journal events in chronological order with timestamps and per-type emoji, and WHEN `--json` is passed THEN the system SHALL emit raw JSONL.

nodes.id: test:test/cli-ux.test.ts::renders the audit trail with per-type emoji
nodes.label: test/cli-ux.test.ts::renders the audit trail with per-type emoji
IF the journal contains an event missing its data payload THEN the system SHALL render the audit trail without crashing.

nodes.id: test:test/robust.test.ts::a structurally-valid event missing `data` does not brick log or the task fold
nodes.label: test/robust.test.ts::a structurally-valid event missing `data` does not brick log or the task fold
progress with emoji after completing a task

WHEN a task transitions to done THEN the system SHALL display the remaining tasks and features with per-task emoji status, per-check proof lights, an overall progress bar with percentage, and the suggested next task.

nodes.id: test:test/cli-ux.test.ts::renders progress with emoji, bar, and next-up
nodes.label: test/cli-ux.test.ts::renders progress with emoji, bar, and next-up

Scenario: empty board shows an explicit empty state, not a broken bar — Given a project with no tasks When the progress view renders Then it states there are no tasks instead of rendering an empty bar

nodes.id: test:test/cli-ux.test.ts::renders an explicit empty state when there are no tasks
nodes.label: test/cli-ux.test.ts::renders an explicit empty state when there are no tasks
IF dev-spec-kit runs outside a dev-spec-kit project THEN the system SHALL NOT create a journal or write any audit event.
config manifest generated from the schema

Scenario: every knob arrives explained — Given the zod config schema When the manifest is generated for a project Then every leaf knob carries section, path, type, default, current value, changed flag, and a non-empty description

nodes.id: test:test/config-manifest.test.ts::every leaf knob is fully described (type, default, value, changed, description)
nodes.label: test/config-manifest.test.ts::every leaf knob is fully described (type, default, value, changed, description)

Scenario: enum and record knobs carry their vocabulary — Given knobs like spec.criteriaFormat and verify.kindRunners When the manifest is generated Then enums list their allowed values and command-records expose the cmd/args record shape

nodes.id: test:test/config-manifest.test.ts::enums carry allowed values; runner records carry the cmd-args shape
nodes.label: test/config-manifest.test.ts::enums carry allowed values; runner records carry the cmd-args shape

Scenario: an undescribed or unsupported knob fails loudly, never silently — Given a schema node the walker does not understand or a leaf without a description When the manifest is generated Then generation throws naming the offending path instead of silently dropping the knob

nodes.id: test:test/config-manifest.test.ts::unsupported or undescribed schema nodes throw with the offending path
nodes.label: test/config-manifest.test.ts::unsupported or undescribed schema nodes throw with the offending path
the RIVET data sidecar is the project's truth

Scenario: the sidecar carries every surface the cockpit renders — Given a project with tasks, requirements, approvals, journal events, and .dev-spec-kit markdown artifacts When the sidecar builds Then window.RIVET contains meta (refreshSeconds, inFlightTasks), dashboard (completion, validates, drift, tasks with failure tails, requirements with per-criterion proofs, approvals, governance, activity) and config (sections + manifest)

nodes.id: test:test/cockpit.test.ts::the RIVET sidecar carries meta, dashboard truth, and the config manifest
nodes.label: test/cockpit.test.ts::the RIVET sidecar carries meta, dashboard truth, and the config manifest

Scenario: stale evidence renders stale, not green — Given a task whose passing result was recorded on an older code tree When the sidecar builds Then that check result is marked stale so the cockpit shows 🟣, never 🟢

nodes.id: test:test/cockpit.test.ts::passing results from an older tree are marked stale in the sidecar
nodes.label: test/cockpit.test.ts::passing results from an older tree are marked stale in the sidecar

Scenario: hostile artifact content cannot escape the script tag — Given a .dev-spec-kit markdown file containing a closing script tag When the sidecar is written Then the payload escapes it so the document cannot be broken or scripted

nodes.id: test:test/cockpit.test.ts::a closing script tag in artifact content is escaped in the sidecar
nodes.label: test/cockpit.test.ts::a closing script tag in artifact content is escaped in the sidecar

Scenario: the cockpit emits shell plus sidecar — Given a dev-spec-kit project When the cockpit is emitted Then .dev-spec-kit/cockpit/ contains index.html, rivet.css, the four JS modules, and rivet.data.js, and index.html loads the sidecar via a script tag

nodes.id: test:test/cockpit.test.ts::emission writes the shell once plus a fresh sidecar
nodes.label: test/cockpit.test.ts::emission writes the shell once plus a fresh sidecar

Scenario: re-emission refreshes data but never rewrites an unchanged shell — Given a cockpit emitted at the current shell version When the cockpit is emitted again Then rivet.data.js is rewritten while the shell files are left untouched And a bumped shell version rewrites the shell

nodes.id: test:test/cockpit.test.ts::re-emission touches only the sidecar until the shell version changes
nodes.label: test/cockpit.test.ts::re-emission touches only the sidecar until the shell version changes
live updates after every proof event

Scenario: live mode keeps the open cockpit current — Given dashboard.updates is "live" When a task completes or a check records Then the sidecar is rewritten so the auto-reloading shell shows the new truth within refreshSeconds

nodes.id: test:test/cockpit.test.ts::live mode rewrites the sidecar on task done and check run
nodes.label: test/cockpit.test.ts::live mode rewrites the sidecar on task done and check run
Scenario: on-demand mode stays quiet — Given dashboard.updates is "on-demand" When a task completes Then no sidecar write happens outside dev-spec-kit dashboard

nodes.id: test:test/cockpit.test.ts::on-demand mode never rewrites the sidecar on task events
nodes.label: test/cockpit.test.ts::on-demand mode never rewrites the sidecar on task events

Scenario: a valid save lands in config.json — Given the cockpit server is running with no tasks in flight When a valid config posts to /api/config Then .dev-spec-kit/config.json is rewritten with the merged config and the save is journaled as governance

nodes.id: test:test/cockpit-server.test.ts::a valid POST saves config.json and journals governance
nodes.label: test/cockpit-server.test.ts::a valid POST saves config.json and journals governance

Scenario: an invalid save returns field errors and writes nothing — Given the cockpit server is running When a config with an invalid enum value posts to /api/config Then the response carries the offending path and message and config.json is unchanged

nodes.id: test:test/cockpit-server.test.ts::an invalid POST returns field errors and never writes
nodes.label: test/cockpit-server.test.ts::an invalid POST returns field errors and never writes

Scenario: GATE-PROTECT refuses saves while tasks are in flight — Given a task is in progress and no unlock window is active When any config posts to /api/config Then the save is refused with GATE-PROTECT-01 and the dev-spec-kit unlock hint and config.json is unchanged

nodes.id: test:test/cockpit-server.test.ts::in-flight tasks refuse the save with GATE-PROTECT-01 and the unlock hint
nodes.label: test/cockpit-server.test.ts::in-flight tasks refuse the save with GATE-PROTECT-01 and the unlock hint
Scenario: the state endpoint serves fresh truth — Given the cockpit server is running When GET /api/state is requested Then the full RIVET object returns with serverMode true

nodes.id: test:test/cockpit-server.test.ts::GET /api/state returns the RIVET object in server mode
nodes.label: test/cockpit-server.test.ts::GET /api/state returns the RIVET object in server mode
every mutation refreshes every generated document

Scenario: a task mutation refreshes all truth surfaces — Given a project whose dashboard updates live When a mutating command runs Then LEDGER.md, TRACKING.md, RESUME.md, graph.json and the cockpit sidecar all reflect the new state

nodes.id: test:test/docs-refresh.test.ts::task mutations refresh boards, resume, graph, and the sidecar
nodes.label: test/docs-refresh.test.ts::task mutations refresh boards, resume, graph, and the sidecar

Scenario: drift re-proofs refresh the documents — Given a stale proof that dev-spec-kit drift re-greens When the drift re-runs complete Then the cockpit sidecar and the boards show the re-greened truth

nodes.id: test:test/docs-refresh.test.ts::drift refreshes the sidecar and boards after re-proving
nodes.label: test/docs-refresh.test.ts::drift refreshes the sidecar and boards after re-proving
Scenario: read-only queries never write documents — Given a project where no cockpit was ever emitted When a read-only query runs Then no document is created or modified — read-only stays read-only

nodes.id: test:test/docs-refresh.test.ts::read-only queries never create or touch documents
nodes.label: test/docs-refresh.test.ts::read-only queries never create or touch documents
Scenario: on-demand opts the sidecar out while boards still refresh — Given dashboard.updates is "on-demand" When a mutating command runs Then the boards refresh while no sidecar is written

nodes.id: test:test/docs-refresh.test.ts::on-demand keeps boards fresh without writing the sidecar
nodes.label: test/docs-refresh.test.ts::on-demand keeps boards fresh without writing the sidecar
a name-filtered run that matches zero tests is never a pass

WHEN a bound check runs a JS test runner (vitest/jest) with a `::name` selector AND the name matches no test THEN the system SHALL record the proof as FAILED, because a run in which zero tests executed proves nothing — an exit-0 "no test matched" is the most corrosive possible false-green.

nodes.id: test:test/report.test.ts::treats a run where 0 tests executed as failed, even on exit 0
nodes.label: test/report.test.ts::treats a run where 0 tests executed as failed, even on exit 0

nodes.id: test:test/runner-trust.test.ts::records a real vitest check whose name matches no test as a FAILED proof
nodes.label: test/runner-trust.test.ts::records a real vitest check whose name matches no test as a FAILED proof
WHEN the named test exists and passes THEN the system SHALL record the proof as PASSED.

nodes.id: test:test/runner-trust.test.ts::records a real vitest check whose name DOES match as a passing proof
nodes.label: test/runner-trust.test.ts::records a real vitest check whose name DOES match as a passing proof
IF the JS runner exits 0 but writes no JSON report THEN the system SHALL refuse to record a proof (treat it as a tooling failure) rather than infer a green it cannot confirm.

nodes.id: test:test/report.test.ts::fails on a non-zero exit even if the report shows no failures (e.g. a crash)
nodes.label: test/report.test.ts::fails on a non-zero exit even if the report shows no failures (e.g. a crash)
flag-like and regex-special test names bind to exactly that test

WHEN a check ref names a test whose name begins with `-` or contains regex metacharacters THEN the system SHALL pass it to the runner as an escaped `--testNamePattern=<value>` (equals form) so the runner's CLI parser binds that exact test and never reads the name as an option.

nodes.id: test:test/runner.test.ts::vitest: a flag-like or regex-special name is escaped into the pattern
nodes.label: test/runner.test.ts::vitest: a flag-like or regex-special name is escaped into the pattern
IF a test name begins with `-` THEN the system SHALL NOT crash the runner CLI (the old `-t <name>` form raised "Unknown option"); the bound check SHALL still resolve to a passing proof.

nodes.id: test:test/runner-trust.test.ts::binds a test whose name begins with '-' without crashing the runner CLI
nodes.label: test/runner-trust.test.ts::binds a test whose name begins with '-' without crashing the runner CLI
one suite run stamps every bound criterion (kills the depth tax)

WHEN `dev-spec-kit verify --stamp` runs THEN the system SHALL execute the test suite ONCE and record a passing/failing `check.run` proof for every bound criterion whose test appears in the run, stamped with the same code tree the suite ran on — so `trace` reads them green without a per-criterion re-run (proving N criteria must cost one run, not N cold `check run`s).

nodes.id: test:test/stamp-batch.test.ts::stamps a file::name ref green from its matching passing test, carrying tree/sha/stack/kind
nodes.label: test/stamp-batch.test.ts::stamps a file::name ref green from its matching passing test, carrying tree/sha/stack/kind

nodes.id: test:test/stamp-batch.test.ts::stamps every binding in one pass (the whole point — N criteria, one run)
nodes.label: test/stamp-batch.test.ts::stamps every binding in one pass (the whole point — N criteria, one run)
IF a bound ref names a test NOT present in the run (another runner, or renamed away) THEN the system SHALL leave that ref's existing proof untouched rather than fabricate or clear it.

nodes.id: test:test/stamp-batch.test.ts::leaves a ref absent from the report UNSTAMPED (it belongs to another runner / run)
nodes.label: test/stamp-batch.test.ts::leaves a ref absent from the report UNSTAMPED (it belongs to another runner / run)
IF a matched test only SKIPPED THEN the system SHALL NOT stamp a proof — skipped is not evidence.

nodes.id: test:test/stamp-batch.test.ts::does NOT stamp a ref whose only match was skipped — skipped is not evidence
nodes.label: test/stamp-batch.test.ts::does NOT stamp a ref whose only match was skipped — skipped is not evidence
static drift check flags orphaned refs before any run

WHEN `dev-spec-kit spec lint` runs THEN the system SHALL resolve every `@check` ref (from specs AND task bindings) against the test files and report any whose file is missing, or whose test name no longer appears in the file, as ORPHANED — exiting non-zero so a Stop/pre-commit hook can refuse the drift.

nodes.id: test:test/spec-lint.test.ts::flags a ref whose file is missing
nodes.label: test/spec-lint.test.ts::flags a ref whose file is missing

nodes.id: test:test/spec-lint.test.ts::flags a ref whose test NAME no longer appears in the file (a rename)
nodes.label: test/spec-lint.test.ts::flags a ref whose test NAME no longer appears in the file (a rename)
WHEN every `@check` ref resolves THEN the system SHALL report a resolvable ref as clean.

nodes.id: test:test/spec-lint.test.ts::passes a ref whose file and name both resolve
nodes.label: test/spec-lint.test.ts::passes a ref whose file and name both resolve
IF a ref is a selector-only form the linter cannot statically resolve (e.g. maven `Class#method`) THEN the system SHALL NOT report it as orphaned — no false positives.

nodes.id: test:test/spec-lint.test.ts::skips a selector-only ref it cannot statically resolve (e.g. maven Class#method)
nodes.label: test/spec-lint.test.ts::skips a selector-only ref it cannot statically resolve (e.g. maven Class#method)
the done-gate tells a stale binding apart from a missing proof

WHEN a task's bound `@check` refs no longer match its requirement's current spec refs THEN the system SHALL report the binding as out of sync — so the done-gate points at `dev-spec-kit spec tasks` (re-sync) instead of telling the user to re-run a ref that no longer exists.

nodes.id: test:test/done-msg.test.ts::is OUT OF sync when a test was renamed (task holds the old ref, spec the new)
nodes.label: test/done-msg.test.ts::is OUT OF sync when a test was renamed (task holds the old ref, spec the new)

nodes.id: test:test/done-msg.test.ts::is out of sync when the counts differ
nodes.label: test/done-msg.test.ts::is out of sync when the counts differ
WHEN a task's bound refs match the spec (order aside) THEN the system SHALL report them in sync, so a genuinely-unproven task is NOT misreported as a binding problem.

nodes.id: test:test/done-msg.test.ts::is in sync when the task's refs match the spec's (order-independent)
nodes.label: test/done-msg.test.ts::is in sync when the task's refs match the spec's (order-independent)
draft-tests scaffolds a failing, bound stub per unbound criterion

WHEN `dev-spec-kit spec draft-tests` runs on a requirement with an unbound criterion THEN the system SHALL emit a FAILING test stub (named from the criterion's clause, carrying the criterion text + the edge-case mandate) plus the `@check` ref that binds it — the rule→test→proof loop the config's `acceptanceCriteria: "tool-drafts"` mode promises but never delivered.

nodes.id: test:test/draft.test.ts::emits a stub that FAILS until implemented and carries the criterion + edge-case mandate
nodes.label: test/draft.test.ts::emits a stub that FAILS until implemented and carries the criterion + edge-case mandate

nodes.id: test:test/draft.test.ts::takes the SHALL clause and drops 'the system'
nodes.label: test/draft.test.ts::takes the SHALL clause and drops 'the system'
IF a criterion already binds a check (or the requirement is an ADR decision record) THEN the system SHALL NOT draft a stub for it — drafting is only for unmet obligations.

nodes.id: test:test/draft.test.ts::drafts only the unbound criterion, skipping bound ones and ADR records
nodes.label: test/draft.test.ts::drafts only the unbound criterion, skipping bound ones and ADR records

WHEN `dev-spec-kit verify --stamp --advance` runs AND a not-done task has a fresh passing proof for every bound check THEN the system SHALL advance that task to done, so `trace` (criteria) and `status` (tasks) stop disagreeing (feedback #7: "trace green while 34 tasks TODO").

nodes.id: test:test/done-msg.test.ts::advances a not-done task whose every check is green on the current tree
nodes.label: test/done-msg.test.ts::advances a not-done task whose every check is green on the current tree
IF any bound check is missing, failing, or proven on an OLDER tree THEN the system SHALL NOT advance the task — only fully and freshly proven work qualifies (it reuses the done-gate's own evidence).

nodes.id: test:test/done-msg.test.ts::does NOT advance a task proven on an OLDER tree (stale)
nodes.label: test/done-msg.test.ts::does NOT advance a task proven on an OLDER tree (stale)
an LLM judge verdict is a recorded, second-class proof
WHEN a `judge` check records a verdict THEN the system SHALL stamp it `kind=judge` carrying the model and reason — labelled distinctly so it is never rendered or counted as an executed green.

nodes.id: test:test/judge.test.ts::records kind=judge with provenance + reason in the tail (never an executed green)
nodes.label: test/judge.test.ts::records kind=judge with provenance + reason in the tail (never an executed green)

nodes.id: test:test/judge.test.ts::respects an explicit mode regardless of the key
nodes.label: test/judge.test.ts::respects an explicit mode regardless of the key
IF no Anthropic API key is present THEN the system SHALL default `auto` mode to harness (the agent supplies the verdict, free) — the common path never requires a key.

nodes.id: test:test/judge.test.ts::auto resolves to api when a key is present, harness when not
nodes.label: test/judge.test.ts::auto resolves to api when a key is present, harness when not

nodes.id: test:test/judge.test.ts::is true only when ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is set
nodes.label: test/judge.test.ts::is true only when ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is set
a circular dependency is flagged, not silently built
WHEN the graph holds a circular `dependsOn` chain THEN the system SHALL report each cycle as a node path and fail the build — a proof loop with no entry point cannot resolve.
WHEN the `dependsOn` edges are acyclic THEN the system SHALL report no cycle, and a non-dependsOn edge SHALL never be mistaken for a dependency cycle — no false positives.

nodes.id: test:test/cycles.test.ts::returns nothing for an acyclic chain
nodes.label: test/cycles.test.ts::returns nothing for an acyclic chain

nodes.id: test:test/cycles.test.ts::ignores non-dependsOn edges (a validates/implements edge is never a dependency cycle)
nodes.label: test/cycles.test.ts::ignores non-dependsOn edges (a validates/implements edge is never a dependency cycle)
the PR body shows the change's blast radius

WHEN a PR body is generated for a set of changed files THEN the system SHALL list, per changed file, the proven graph edges it touches — a changed test file via the `validates` edge it proves, a changed source file via its code node — so a reviewer sees the diff's traceability impact without the graph.

nodes.id: test:test/pr-blast.test.ts::maps a changed TEST file to the validates edge it proves
nodes.label: test/pr-blast.test.ts::maps a changed TEST file to the validates edge it proves

nodes.id: test:test/pr-blast.test.ts::renders the touched edges for changed files that map
nodes.label: test/pr-blast.test.ts::renders the touched edges for changed files that map

IF none of the changed files map to a graph node THEN the system SHALL say so honestly rather than claim zero impact, and IF the change set is unknown or empty THEN the system SHALL NOT render the section at all.

nodes.id: test:test/pr-blast.test.ts::notes honestly when changed files map to no graph node
nodes.label: test/pr-blast.test.ts::notes honestly when changed files map to no graph node

nodes.id: test:test/pr-blast.test.ts::omits the section entirely when changedFiles is undefined (back-compat)
nodes.label: test/pr-blast.test.ts::omits the section entirely when changedFiles is undefined (back-compat)
proven implements edges tie changed source files to their requirements

WHEN the Verified Traceability Graph is built with a code graph THEN the system SHALL emit a proven `implements` edge from each source file a requirement's bound test imports to that requirement — so a changed SOURCE file's blast radius and the `unimplementedRequirements` check stop being empty (closing the gap FEAT-BLAST-01 left: source files mapped to nothing because only `validates` edges existed).

nodes.id: test:test/implements-edges.test.ts::links a source file a bound test imports to the requirement, carrying its rollup proof
nodes.label: test/implements-edges.test.ts::links a source file a bound test imports to the requirement, carrying its rollup proof

nodes.id: test:test/implements-edges.test.ts::buildVTG emits a green implements edge that makes unimplementedRequirements live
nodes.label: test/implements-edges.test.ts::buildVTG emits a green implements edge that makes unimplementedRequirements live

nodes.id: test:test/implements-edges.test.ts::lights up a changed source file's blast radius through the implements edge
nodes.label: test/implements-edges.test.ts::lights up a changed source file's blast radius through the implements edge
WHEN a requirement is not fully proven THEN the system SHALL carry the WORST criterion proof onto its implements edges, so an `implements` edge is never greener than the executed checks behind it.

nodes.id: test:test/implements-edges.test.ts::inherits the requirement's worst criterion proof — green only when every criterion is green
nodes.label: test/implements-edges.test.ts::inherits the requirement's worst criterion proof — green only when every criterion is green

IF a requirement's tests import only a TEST file, or import nothing the code graph indexes THEN the system SHALL NOT fabricate an implements edge — a test is never an implementation, and an unanchored requirement stays flagged as unimplemented.

nodes.id: test:test/implements-edges.test.ts::never links a test→test import as an implementation
nodes.label: test/implements-edges.test.ts::never links a test→test import as an implementation

nodes.id: test:test/implements-edges.test.ts::does not link a source the requirement's tests never import
nodes.label: test/implements-edges.test.ts::does not link a source the requirement's tests never import

edges.from: test:test/cli-ux.test.ts::audits cli invocations into the journal

lastCheck.ref: test/cli-ux.test.ts::does not create journals outside dev-spec-kit projects
